release.document_title: National Interoperability Field Operations Guide Version 2.02
release.permitted_use: Public field reference material. Structured records remain subject to the authorization, eligibility, coordination, and usage conditions in the source document.
release.transformation_method: Deterministic extraction of the interoperability channel tables on printed pages 28-59 and the SAR command plan on printed page 63, followed by record-count, schema, checksum, and visual sample review.

conventional_channels.name: 7-US-01
conventional_channels.authorization: The NIFOG is reference material and does not itself authorize operation. Use requires applicable FCC or NTIA authority and compliance with the conditions in NIFOG 2.02.
conventional_channels.restrictions:
LICENSING REQUIRED: These frequencies are NOT covered by the “Blanket Authorization” for nationwide interoperability channels. Use of these channels must be licensed or authorized by STA.
LICENSING NOTES: These channels may be licensed for national itinerant mobile use, use station class MOI. These
channels may be used in either Analog or Digital mode and are limited to 2 watts (ERP).
- Licensees are responsible for the administration of these channels.
- Utilize normal squelch in programming which will use the default talkgroup - $0001 (110).

conventional_channels.name: 7AG58
conventional_channels.authorization: The NIFOG is reference material and does not itself authorize operation. Use requires applicable FCC or NTIA authority and compliance with the conditions in NIFOG 2.02.
conventional_channels.restrictions:
LICENSING REQUIRED: These frequencies are NOT covered by the “Blanket Authorization” for nationwide interoperability channels. Use of these channels must be licensed or authorized by STA.
TX NAC: $293 (65910). RX NAC $F7E (396610). These channels are reserved for air-ground communications to be used
by low-altitude aircraft and ground-based stations: See FCC rule 90.531(7). (i) Airborne use of these channels is limited to
aircraft flying at or below 457 meters (1500 feet) above ground level. (ii) Aircraft are limited to 2 watts effective radiated
power (ERP) when transmitting while airborne on these channels. (iii) Aircraft may transmit on either the mobile or base
transmit side of the channel pair. (iv) States are responsible for the administration of these channels.
- Utilize normal squelch in programming which will use the default talkgroup - $0001 (110).

conventional_channels.name: 7CALL50
conventional_channels.authorization: The NIFOG is reference material and does not itself authorize operation. Use requires applicable FCC or NTIA authority and compliance with the conditions in NIFOG 2.02.
conventional_channels.restrictions:
TX NAC: $293 (65910)
“$” indicates hexadecimal value, “10” subscript indicates decimal value.
- Authorized emission – 8K10F1E (Digital P25 Phase I Modulation) – 47 CFR §90.548(a)(1)
- Utilize normal squelch in programming which will use the default talkgroup - $0001 (110).
- AES Encryption permitted on all but the two nationwide interoperability calling channels. Must have accessible switch
or other readily accessible control that permits the radio user to disable encryption. 47 CFR §90.553
*Recommended as PRIMARY calling channel for 700 MHz Band.

conventional_channels.name: VTAC17
conventional_channels.authorization: The NIFOG is reference material and does not itself authorize operation. Use requires applicable FCC or NTIA authority and compliance with the conditions in NIFOG 2.02.
conventional_channels.source_section: VHF National Interoperability Channels - VHF Inland – VTAC17
conventional_channels.restrictions:
LICENSING REQUIRED: These frequencies are NOT covered by the “Blanket Authorization” for nationwide interoperability channels. Use of these channels must be licensed or authorized by STA.
For VTAC17/VTAC17D only: Base stations: 50 watts max, antenna HAAT 400 feet max. Mobile stations: 20 watts max, antenna HAAT 15 feet max. These channels are for tactical use and may not be operated on board aircraft in flight. These channels use 2.5 kHz deviation narrowband Analog FM and are available only in certain inland areas at least 100 miles from a major waterway. These channels use the same frequencies as VHF Marine channel 25, which uses 5 kHz deviation wideband Analog FM. Use only where authorized. See map on next page. In these authorized areas, interoperability communications have priority over grandfathered public coast and public safety licensees. See 47 CFR §90.20(g)(3). - Authorized emission – 11K0F3E (2.5 kHz deviation narrowband analog FM) - Limited to 3 watts ERP North of Line A or East of Line C.
